paragraphs: Cohort Hierarchy + Persona Mapping Specification
DOC-03 | Version 1.0 | Meal Planning RE Technical Docs v1
1. Purpose and Scope
Defines how user-facing main cohorts and sub-cohorts map to detailed backend personas and overlays. The purpose is to keep onboarding simple while retaining a rich machine-facing persona system.

This document is part of the canonical 28-document technical system. It should be read in the order defined in DOC-00. It is written for an AI coding agent that must implement the agreed recommendation engine exactly, without inventing a competing architecture.
2. Applicable Build Series
3. Core Implementation Invariants
Always build a household profile before generating any plan.
Always map user-facing cohort answers into backend personas and overlays; do not expose all backend personas in onboarding.
Always generate a 7-day class-first plan before selecting dishes.
Always keep primary family meal classes separate from member-specific add-on components.
Never allow kid, elderly, pregnancy, postpartum, diabetic, or recovery add-on classes to replace the main family meal unless the household itself has only that member segment.
Always apply hard constraints before scoring: diet type, allergies, Jain/fasting rules, and Never list.
Always use home state and current city as separate signals; do not collapse them into one location field.
Always use class-to-dish mapping keyed by meal_class_code; never mix dishes from unrelated meal classes.
Use v3 workbook IDs as canonical seed IDs unless a later approved source-data version supersedes them.
4. User-facing Cohort Design

Onboarding should show only a small set of main cohorts, not the full backend persona list. The v3 workbook defines 5 main cohorts and 41 backend personas/subcohorts. The user should feel the app asks natural questions, not a taxonomy quiz.
5. Backend Persona Mapping Rules
Map main cohort selection to a limited sub-cohort screen.
Map sub-cohort to a base backend persona.
Then ask targeted follow-ups for overlays: baby/toddler, school child, elderly, diabetes, pregnancy, postpartum, fitness, cook dependency, non-veg cadence.
Allow multiple overlays where real households overlap, for example family with toddler plus diabetic elder.
Store both base persona and overlay personas in the user profile.
6. Persona Confidence Model

Every persona assignment should carry a confidence score. High confidence comes from explicit sub-cohort selection plus supporting answers. Medium confidence comes from main cohort plus household members. Low confidence comes from skipped onboarding and should use DOC-20 cold-start assumptions.
7. Overlap Handling
8. Implementation Output

persona_assignment = {  "main_cohort_id": "MC_FAMILY",  "sub_cohort_id": "SC_COUPLE_WITH_INFANT",  "base_persona_id": "P_COUPLE_WITH_INFANT",  "overlay_persona_ids": ["O_DIABETIC_ELDER"],  "confidence": 0.86,  "routing_trace": ["main_cohort", "sub_cohort", "health_overlay"]}
9. AI Coding Agent Instructions
Read DOC-00 first to understand build sequence and document dependencies.
Use this document when implementing: BUILD-02, BUILD-03.
Treat all IDs in source workbook v3 as canonical seed data identifiers.
When logic and data conflict, follow the rule hierarchy: hard constraints > add-on separation > weekly class matrix > state/city overlay > dish scoring > randomization.
Do not create new meal class codes, persona codes, or enum values in application code. Additions must go through DOC-27 Admin CMS and DOC-28 governance.
Write unit tests based on the acceptance criteria in this document and DOC-25.
10. Acceptance Criteria
User is never shown a long backend persona list.
Every detailed persona used by the engine can be traced to onboarding answers.
Overlapping personas are supported through overlays rather than duplicate persona explosion.
11. Change Log

Then ask baby age if selected, food restrictions, nonveg frequency, cook availability, origin-current city.
Children-specific food becomes add-on component; main plan remains family meal.
Elder/recovery food becomes add-on component unless the whole household follows it.
Special goal or kitchen operating mode
This can be selected first or captured as overlay after MC1-MC4. Do not force user to browse all detailed personas.

Attach infant add-on and diabetic elder add-on; primary family meal remains family class.
Working couple + no cook + fitness goal
Use no-cook/quick cook capability plus high-protein/light overlay.
Family with school child + Jain restriction
Use child add-on preferences only within Jain constraints.
If the whole household is elderly, soft classes may be primary; otherwise soft food is add-on.
